body_preview: Add `exit_code` to the `CommandCompleted` state event payload so that bash command failures (non-zero exit codes) are visible in state evidence, not just in transcripts. This closes the gap where the dashboard detects failed bash commands in transcripts that appear as successful tool calls in state events. Why this matters: The trajectory reports `transcript
expected_evidence: Future dashboard runs show `transcript_only_failed_tool_count` decreasing as bash failures become visible in state events. The `failed_tool_summary.bash_tool_error` metric in state graphs can be computed from state events rather than requiring transcript parsing. Implementation Notes: The change location is `src/prompt.rs`, around lines 869-877, inside the `
title: Capture bash exit_code in CommandCompleted state events to close transcript-state gap
